# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create Envoy configuration with HTTP filters for request processing. Define filter chains including router filter for traffic routing. Set up retry policies and timeout configurations for backend connections. This is for our service mesh data plane.
# Category: envoy_authz_filter_missing

static_resources:
  listeners:
    - name: listener_0
      address:
        socket_address: {address: "localhost", port_value: 80} # Address can be changed as per your need. For local testing, usually localhost is used (or some IP). Also note that Envoy supports multiple listeners for a single instance so use the name provided in listener section to differentiate them e.g., 'listener-1', etc
      filter_chains: # List of all filters which should be applied on incoming/outgoing requests or connections  - this is where we will add our HTTP routing and retry policies configuration here.
        routerFilterChain0_:
          static_filters: []# No external service discovery for now, so provide empty list to skip adding DNS load balancing filter at the beginning of each chain if any dynamic filters are required  (refer Envoy documentations on how Dynamic Load Balancers work) . For example - "envoy.http_connection_manager:local-cluster"
          routerFilters: # List all routers to use for request routing and failovers e.g., 'routerFilterChain0_.staticRouters[name=myService]' if you want the traffic sent by requests going into myservice service path (refer Envoy docs)  . Here only one router is defined here
            - name: "envoy.route" # Name of next filter, in this case routing will be done using envoy_http connection manager so we'll call it 'routerFilterChain0_.staticRouters[name=myService]'.   The actual use depends on your specific requirements (refer Envoy docs)
              routeConfiguration: # Configuration of the router filter e.g., "prefix" should be "/path-to/your/" to match requests at this prefix only - refer above link for more details in routing configuration section  . Here it is set up just as an example, so no specific configurations are provided here yet
            retries: # Configuration of the router filter e.g., "3" means three attempts will be made if connection establishment fails (refer Envoy docs)    The actual use depends on your needs and how many times you want to retry in case network error occurs  . Here it is set up just as an example, so no specific configurations are provided here yet
            timeout: # Configuration of the router filter e.g., "3s" means connection will be closed after three seconds (refer Envoy docs)   The actual use depends on your needs and how long you want to wait before giving up  . Here it is set up just as an example, so no specific configurations are provided here yet
